required_inputs: request intent and intended action
privacy, legal, HIM, security, clinical, payer, and compliance authority map
specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal advice, final breach determination, formal certification, or unsupported disclosure approval
request asks to bypass identity verification, minimum-necessary, consent, restriction, role-based access, or audit controls
request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical judgment, payer policy interpretation, or authorization approval

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, provider operating, and recognized workflow sources
tenant policy overlays, source hierarchy, and jurisdictional context
source freshness metadata
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant source context is missing
HIPAA, Part 2, state law, tenant policy, facility rule, or payer/program rule conflicts materially

required_inputs: authorization forms, disclosure logs, ROI records, EHR audit logs, IAM records, incident notes, training attestations, and policy references
patient/member, requester, recipient, purpose, PHI scope, date range, service setting, and owner map
audit, retention, disclosure, incident, and closure evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical privacy artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from request or incident to source record, owner, and closure evidence cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across privacy incident management, EHR audit logging, IAM, ROI, patient portal, case management, policy, training, legal, security, and payer systems
dependency owners and routing matrix
authorization, consent, identity, restriction, accounting, minimum-necessary, eligibility, referral, and documentation prerequisites
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on patient identity, requester authority, authorization status, access event, disclosure log, or incident closure state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
PHI exposure, clinical safety, payer, legal, or breach indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change privacy handling
PHI handling, clinical risk, payer interpretation, legal interpretation, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to legal, HIM/ROI, security incident response, IAM, patient access, compliance, licensed clinical, payer operations, utilization management, or records retention ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: privacy request or incident class
HIPAA/OCR, CMS, ONC, NIST, HL7, AHIMA, and tenant-policy source context
evidence owners, control owners, review cadence, and applicable caveats
refusal_triggers: governing obligation cannot be mapped to a source or tenant policy
mapping would require legal interpretation, final signoff, or regulator-facing position

required_inputs: required evidence packet for disclosure, access, accounting, incident, training, or remediation review
review cadence, owner map, retention expectation, and system references
source traceability and policy baseline
refusal_triggers: required evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or unavailable
review request expects final signoff without authorized owner decision

required_inputs: exception type and severity
control gap, owner, due date, remediation evidence, and residual risk
PHI, patient-safety, legal, security, workforce, and payer impact indicators
refusal_triggers: closure is requested without remediation evidence or owner decision
PHI, legal, clinical, payer, security, or workforce risk remains unresolved
sanctions, breach, legal, or executive risk acceptance decision is required

required_inputs: requested signoff or approval
authority matrix
required evidence and responsible owner
refusal_triggers: request asks for formal legal, breach, regulator, disclosure, clinical, payer, sanctions, or executive signoff
request asks to mark control, incident, or disclosure issue closed without authorized owner approval

required_inputs: request action class
clinical risk and patient-safety signals
licensed owner escalation path
tenant clinical escalation policy
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, triage, treatment plan, medication instruction, clinical record judgment, or symptom interpretation
patient-safety or clinical-risk signal changes privacy handling

required_inputs: PHI request or incident details
consent, authorization, personal representative, restriction, minimum-necessary, role-based access, and communication constraints
patient-safety, breach, clinical, payer, and legal indicators
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure authority is missing, stale, disputed, or conflicting
clinical risk, patient-safety signal, breach-sensitive event, or payer interpretation changes the privacy answer
privacy or legal policy interpretation is required

notes: All shared abilities, all compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and all healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are mapped to one or more scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario coverage.

Coverage includes authorization and identity gaps, breach-sensitive evidence gaps, minimum-necessary refusal, legal signoff refusal, clinical and payer escalation, tenant adaptation, and policy conflict handling.
